---
title: "Lightweight 4Dpapers Example"
format: html
---

## Lightweight Electrophysiology Example

This example keeps the rendered output smaller than the full heart example. It
shows the Niederer slab case, the Purkinje network, and a Plotly graph without
embedding the large full-heart mesh.

### Niederer Slab

The Niederer benchmark is a compact slab geometry. This figure shows the
transmembrane potential field at the final timestep and exposes the field
switcher for `Vm` and `activationTime`.

{{< 4d-image id="niederer-light"
    src="data/Niederer.foam"
    field="Vm"
    fields="Vm,activationTime"
    time="last"
    caption="Niederer slab transmembrane potential at the final timestep." >}}

### Field Comparison

The same slab can be shown in a synchronized panel, comparing `Vm` with
activation time while sharing the camera between views.

{{< 4d-panel id="niederer-light-panel" layout="2x1" height="520px" camera="sync"
    caption="Niederer slab: Vm and activationTime with synchronized cameras."
    src1="data/Niederer.foam"
    id1="niederer-light-vm"
    field1="Vm"
    src2="data/Niederer.foam"
    id2="niederer-light-at"
    field2="activationTime" >}}

### Time-Series Strip

For PDF-friendly summaries, the time-series shortcode renders representative
timesteps as a strip while keeping the HTML version interactive.

{{< 4d-timeseries id="niederer-light-ts"
    src="data/Niederer.foam"
    field="Vm"
    fields="Vm,activationTime"
    steps="4"
    height="340px"
    caption="Niederer slab Vm over four representative timesteps." >}}

## Purkinje Network

The Purkinje network example uses the 1D conduction network only. This avoids
the large myocardial mesh while still showing a time-varying electrophysiology
dataset.

{{< 4d-image id="purkinje-light"
    src="data/monodomainHeart/postProcessing/purkinjeNetworkVTK/purkinjeNetwork.vtk.series"
    field="Vm_V"
    cmap="plasma"
    decimate="none"
    stride="8"
    time="5"
    caption="Purkinje network transmembrane potential Vm_V." >}}

## Plotly Graph

Plotly JSON files can be embedded with the graph shortcode. In HTML they remain
interactive; in PDF they are rasterized for a static export.

{{< 4d-graph
    src="media/example_graph.json"
    id="fig-light-plotly"
    caption="Example Plotly surface embedded from JSON." >}}
